zh.lifeSummary: 當Benjamin Yerxa White II於1847年1月13日出生在加拿大新布隆士威省約克弗雷德里克頓時，他的父親Benjamin Yerxa White I33歲，母親Margaret Jones27歲。他於1879年10月5日與Effie Mae Colbroth在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1880年住在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福且在美國威斯康辛州索耶辛特 蕾克住了大約5年。他去世於1918年5月15日在美國明尼蘇達州聖 路易度盧特享年71歲，埋葬在美國威斯康辛州索耶史東 蕾克史東 蕾克 艾福格林 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Benjamin Yerxa White II于1847年1月13日出生在加拿大新布隆士威省約克弗雷德里克頓时，他的父亲Benjamin Yerxa White I33岁，母亲Margaret Jones27岁。他于1879年10月5日与Effie Mae Colbroth在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他于1880年住在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福且在美国威斯康辛州索耶辛特 蕾克住了大约5年。他去世于1918年5月15日在美国明尼苏达州聖 路易度盧特享年71岁，埋葬在美国威斯康辛州索耶史東 蕾克史東 蕾克 艾福格林 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Benjamin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the youngest of the twelve sons of Jacob. His mother Rachel died in giving birth to him, and in her last moments she named him Benoni, meaning ‘son of my sorrow’. His father, however, did not wish him to bear such an ill-omened name, and renamed him Benyamin (Genesis 35:16–18; 42:4). This means either ‘son of the right hand’ or more likely ‘son of the south’ (Hebrew yamin can also mean ‘south’), since Benjamin was the only child of Jacob born in Canaan and not in Mesopotamia to the north. Another tradition is that the second element of the name is a variant of the Hebrew plural noun yamim, which means ‘days’ but is used idiomatically to mean ‘year’ or ‘years’. The name would then mean ‘son of (my) old age’ and refer to the fact that Benjamin was Jacob's youngest child. In the Middle Ages the name was often given to sons whose mothers had died in childbirth. Today it has no such unfortunate associations and it grew enormously in popularity following the release of the film The Graduate ( 1967 ), in which Dustin Hoffman played the role of Benjamin Braddock . It is used in Scotland as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Beathan .

nameMeanings.name: Yerxa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Jurckse, a patronymic meaning ‘son of George’. The surname Jurckse is not found in the Netherlands.
History: Paulus Jurckse (1630–1682) arrived in New Netherland (now NY) in 1651 on Den Houttuyn, coming from Texel, the Netherlands.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1850: Itasca, Minnesota Territory, United States 1854: Newton, Minnesota Territory, United States 1856: St. Louise, Minnesota Territory, United States 1858: St. Louise, Minnesota, United States

historicEvents.title: The Creation of the Republican Party
historicEvents.description: A debate continues over the location of the creation of the Republican Party. Some sources claim that the party was formed in Ripon, Wisconsin, on February 28, 1854. Others claim the first meeting of the Republican Party took place in Jackson, Michigan, on July 6, 1854, where the Republican Party was officially organized. Over 1,000 people were present and candidates were selected for the party, thus making it the first Republican convention.

historicEvents.title: The First Civil Rights Act
historicEvents.description: The first federal law that defined what was citizenship and affirm that all citizens are equally protected by the law. Its main objective was to protect the civil rights of persons of African descent.

historicEvents.title: British North America Act
historicEvents.description: The British North America Act or Constitution Act of 1867 caused three British colonies, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, and Canada to be united as one under the name Canada. Until this point New Brunswick had been the British crown colony.

historicEvents.title: Iron Mining starts in Minnesota
historicEvents.description: After discovering iron ore in the Vermilion Range in North-East of Minnesota, iron mining companies began to come to the area and caused an economic boom to the area of Duluth and to the state as a whole.

historicEvents.title: So Much Farm Land
historicEvents.description: A law that funded many irrigation and agricultural projects in the western states.

historicEvents.title: Squaw Mountain Fire Lookout
historicEvents.description: In 1905, a fire lookout tower (among the first of its kind) was constructed in Piscataquis County on Squaw Mountain. Due to Maine's abundance of lumber and paper mills, it was essential that a system be developed to help spot fires as quickly as possible.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Maine Historical Society Online - Looking Out: Maine's Fire Towers

en.lifeSummary: When Benjamin Yerxa White II was born on 13 January 1847, in Fredericton, York, New Brunswick, Canada, his father, Benjamin Yerxa White I, was 33 and his mother, Margaret Jones, was 27. He married Effie Mae Colbroth on 5 October 1879, in Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He lived in Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, United States in 1880 and Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United States for about 5 years. He died on 15 May 1918, in Duluth, St. Louis, Minnesota, United States, at the age of 71, and was buried in Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Benjamin Yerxa White II est né le 13 janvier 1847, à Fredericton, York, Nouveau-Brunswick, Canada, son père, Benjamin Yerxa White I, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margaret Jones, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Effie Mae Colbroth le 5 octobre 1879, à Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il a vécu à Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis en 1880 et à Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, États-Unis pendant environ 5 ans. Il est décédé le 15 mai 1918, à Duluth, St. Louis, Minnesota, États-Unis, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Benjamin Yerxa White II am 13. Januar 1847 in Fredericton, York, New Brunswick, Kanada geboren wurde, war sein Vater Benjamin Yerxa White I 33 und seine Mutter Margaret Jones 27. Er hat am 5. Oktober 1879 in Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten Effie Mae Colbroth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. 1880 hat er in Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt, und ungefähr 5 Jahre lang hat er in Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 15. Mai 1918 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Duluth, St. Louis, Minnesota, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Benjamin Yerxa White II è nato il il 13 gennaio 1847 nel Fredericton, York, Nuovo Brunswick, Canada, il suo padre, Benjamin Yerxa White I, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Jones, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Effie Mae Colbroth il 5 ottobre 1879, nel Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Vissuto a Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1880 e nel Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 5 anni. Morì il 15 maggio 1918, nel Duluth, St. Louis, Minnesota, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Benjamin Yerxa White IIが1847年1月13日にカナダニューブランズウィック州ヨークフレデリクトンで生まれた時，父Benjamin Yerxa White Iは33歳，母Margaret Jonesは27歳であった。1879年10月5日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクルドロウでEffie Mae Colbrothと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1880年にはアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクルドロウに，アメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州サウヤーサンド ラクに約5年間住んでいた。彼は1918年5月15日にアメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州スト. ロウイスダルスで71歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州サウヤーストーン ラクストーン ラク エバーグレン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Benjamin Yerxa White II(이)가 1847년 1월 13일 캐나다 뉴브런즈윅 요르크 프레더릭턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benjamin Yerxa White I(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Jones(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1879년 10월 5일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 루들로에서 Effie Mae Colbroth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1880년도에 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 루들로에서, 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 상드 레이크에서 약 5년 동안 살았다. 그는 1918년 5월 15일 미국 미네소타 주 스트. 루이스 드루스에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 스톤 레이크 스톤 레이크 에버그린 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Benjamin Yerxa White II nasceu em 13 de janeiro de 1847, em Fredericton, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá, seu pai, Benjamin Yerxa White I, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Jones, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Effie Mae Colbroth em 5 de outubro de 1879, em Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele viveu em Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América em 1880 e em Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América por quase 5 anos. Ele faleceu em 15 de maio de 1918, em Duluth, St. Louis, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Benjamin Yerxa White II родился 13 января 1847 г. в местности фредириктон, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада, его отцу по имени Benjamin Yerxa White I было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Jones, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Effie Mae Colbroth 5 октября 1879 г. в местности лудлоу, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он жил в местности лудлоу, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в 1880 году и в местности сэнд леик, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты около 5 лет. Он умер 15 мая 1918 г. в местности дулут, ст. луиз, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности стоун леик эвиргрин кеметири, стоун леик, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Benjamin Yerxa White II nació el 13 de enero de 1847, en Fredericton, York, Nuevo Brunswick, Canadá, su papá, Benjamin Yerxa White I, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Margaret Jones, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Effie Mae Colbroth el 5 de octubre de 1879, en Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Vivió en Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos en 1880 y en Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos durante unos 5 años. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1918, en Duluth, St. Louis, Minnesota, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Stone Lake Evergreen Cemetery, Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos.
